policy: General System Security Profile forSUSE Linux Enterprise Micro (SLEM) 5
title: General System Security Profile SUSE Linux Enterprise Micro (SLEM) 5

controls.title: Ensure /tmp Located On Separate Partition
Add nodev Option to /tmp
Add nosuid Option to /tmp
Configure /dev/shm
Add noexec Option to /dev/shm
Add nodev Option to /dev/shm
Add nosuid Option to /dev/shm
Ensure /var Located On Separate Partition
Ensure /var/log/audit Located On Separate Partition
Ensure /home Located On Separate Partition
Ensure /var Located On Separate Partition
Add nodev Option to /home
Add noexec Option to Removable Media Partitions
Add nodev Option to Removable Media Partitions
Add nosuid Option to Removable Media Partitions
Verify that All World-Writable Directories Have Sticky Bits Set
Disable Modprobe Loading of USB Storage Driver
Configure GPG keys
Configure package manager repositories
Ensure gpgcheck Enabled In Main zypper Configuration
Install sudo Package
Ensure Only Users Logged In To Real tty Can Execute Sudo - sudo use_pty
Ensure Sudo Logfile Exists - sudo logfile
Configure grup.cfg Group/User Ownership and Permissions
The AIDE package must be installed if it is to be available for integrity checking
Configure Systemd Timer Execution of AIDE
Set the Boot Loader Password
Require Authentication for Emergency and Single User mode
Enable NX/XD Support
Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
Modify the System Message of the Day Banner
Modify the System Login Banner
Modify the System Login Banner for Remote Connections
Verify Ownership and Permissions of/on Message of the Day Banner
Verify Ownership and Permissions of/on System Login Banner
Verify Ownership and Permissions of/on System Login Banner for Remote Connections
Ensure Software Patches Installed
The xinetd package is uninstalled, and its service is disabled
The Chrony package is installed
Configure the Chrony
Uninstall Avahi Server
Uninstall CUPS
Uninstall DHCP Server
Uninstall openldap-servers
Uninstall nfs-utils
Uninstall rpcbind
Uninstall bind
Uninstall vsftpd
Uninstall dovecot (IMAP/POP3)
Uninstall samba
Uninstall net-snmp
Uninstall rsync
Uninstall telnet-server Package
Uninstall rsh
Uninstall talk
Uninstall nonessential services
Disable IPv6
Deactivate Wireless Network Interfaces
Disable IP Forwarding
Disable Packet Redirect Sending
Disable forwarding source-routed packets
Disable accepting Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects
Disable Kernel Parameter for Accepting ICMP Redirects by Default on IPv4 Interfaces
Log suspicious packets on all IPv4 interfaces
Enable Kernel Parameter to Ignore ICMP Broadcast Echo Requests on IPv4 Interfaces
Enable Kernel Parameter to Ignore Bogus ICMP Error Responses on IPv4 Interfaces
Enable Reverse Path Filtering on all IPv4 Interfaces
Enable Kernel Parameter to Use TCP Syncookies on Network Interfaces
Disable Accepting Router Advertisements on all IPv6 Interfaces
Disable SCTP Support
Install firewalld
Verify firewalld Enabled
Set Default firewalld Zone for Incoming Packets
Ensure firewalld network interfaces are assigned to appropriate zone
Ensure firewalld Unnecessary Services and Ports Are Not Accepted
Ensure the audit Subsystem is Installed
Enable auditd Service
Configure auditd Max Log File Size
Configure auditd max_log_file_action Upon Reaching Maximum Log Size
Configure auditd actions on Low Disk Space
Record attempts to modify the date and time
Collect events that modify user/group information
Record Events that Modify the System's Network Environment
Record Events that Modify the System''s Mandatory Access Control
Collect login and logout events
Record Attempts to Alter Process and Session Initiation Information
Collect discretionary access control permission modification events
Record Unsuccessful Access Attempts to Files - open

controls.notes: Additional rules for priviliged commands are available and can be enabled.
controls.title: Ensure auditd Collects Information on the Use of Privileged Commands
Ensure auditd Collects Information on Exporting to Media (successful)
Ensure auditd Collects File Deletion Events by Users
Ensure auditd Collects System Administrator Actions
Record Attempts to perform maintenance activities
Collect kernel module loading and unloading
Make the auditd Configuration Immutable
Configure systemd-journal-remote to send logs to a remote log host
Verify permissions of log files
Verify Ownership and Permissions of/on SSH Server config file
Verify Permissions on SSH Server Private *_key Key Files
Verify Permissions on SSH Server Public *.pub Key Files
Limit Users' SSH Access
Set SSH Daemon LogLevel to VERBOSE
Disable X11 Forwarding
Set SSH authentication attempt limit
Disable SSH Support for .rhosts Files
Disable Host-Based Authentication
Disable SSH Root Login
Disable SSH Access via Empty Passwords
Do Not Allow SSH Environment Options
Use only strong Ciphers
Use only strong MAC algorithms
Use Only Strong Key Exchange algorithms
Configure SSH Idle Timeout Interval
Ensure SSH LoginGraceTime is configured
Configure SSH warning banner
Enable SSH PAM
Disable SSH TCP Forwarding
Ensure SSH MaxStartups is configured
Set SSH MaxSessions limit
Configure password creation requirements
Set Deny For Failed Password Attempts
Limit Password Reuse
Verify All Account Password Hashes are Shadowed with SHA512
Set Existing Passwords Maximum Age
Set Existing Passwords Minimum Age
Set and Apply Password Warning Age
Set Account Expiration Following Inactivity
Ensure all users last password change date is in the past
Ensure that System Accounts Do Not Run a Shell Upon Login
Verify Root Has A Primary GID 0
Set Interactive Session Timeout
Ensure the Default Umask is Set Correctly in login.defs
Restrict direct and virtual console Root Logins
Enforce Usage of pam_wheel on the System for su Authentication
Verify and Correct Ownership and File Permissions with RPM
Configure permissions on /etc/passwd
Configure permissions on /etc/shadow
Configure permissions on /etc/group
Configure permissions on /etc/passwd-
Configure permissions on /etc/shadow-
Configure permissions on /etc/group-
Ensure No World-Writable Files Exist
Ensure All Files Are Owned by a Use
Ensure All Files Are Owned by a Group
Ensure All SUID Executables Are Authorized
Ensure All SGID Executables Are Authorized
Verify All Account Password Hashes are Shadowed
Ensure there are no legacy NIS entries in /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow
Verify Only Root Has UID 0
Ensure Root's path Integrity
All Interactive Users Home Directories Must Exist
All Interactive User Home Directories Must Have mode 0750 Or Less Permissive
All Interactive User Home Directories Must Be Group-Owned By The Primary Group
User Initialization Files Must Not Run World-Writable Programs
Verify No .forward Files Exist
Verify No netrc Files Exist
Ensure users' .netrc Files are not group or world accessible
Remove Rsh Trust Files
Ensure all GIDs referenced in /etc/passwd are defined in /etc/group
Ensure All Accounts on the System Have Unique User IDs
Ensure All Groups on the System Have Unique Group ID
Ensure All Accounts on the System Have Unique Names
Ensure All Groups on the System Have Unique Group Names
Ensure shadow Group on the System is Empty
